Home / OpenAI Token Calculator

OpenAI Token Calculator

Compare OpenAI API costs across 14 models — GPT-5.5 to GPT-5 Nano, GPT-4.1 family, and o-series. Toggle batch pricing, prompt caching, and growth projections.

🎭 Scenario Presets
📝 Token Estimator
Estimated: ~<span id="est-tokens">0</span> input tokens
⚙️ Advanced Options
Available: gpt-5.5, gpt-5.2, gpt-5, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano, gpt-4.1, gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano, o3, o4-mini, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo, gpt-3.5-turbo

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter the model keys you want to compare (comma-separated, e.g. "gpt-5-mini,gpt-5.5,gpt-4.1").
2
Enter your average input and output tokens per request. Use the token estimator to gauge your prompt sizes.
3
Set your daily request volume and choose real-time or batch pricing mode.
4
Optionally set a cache hit rate and monthly growth rate for cost projections.
5
Review the detail cards for each selected model — compare per-request, daily, monthly, and annual costs.
6
Use the Usage Scenarios table to see how costs scale from 50 to 10,000 requests/day.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I estimate token counts for my prompts?

Roughly 1 token = 0.75 English words (4 characters). For CJK languages (Chinese, Japanese, Korean), 1 character ~= 1.5 tokens. A 1000-word English article uses ~1,300 tokens. Use the built-in token estimator to paste text and get an instant count.

Which model is cheapest?

GPT-5 Nano is the cheapest at $0.05/$0.40 per 1M input/output tokens. For simple classification, summarization, and high-volume Q&A, it can be 100x cheaper than GPT-5.5. GPT-4.1 Nano ($0.10/$0.40) is also excellent for budget workloads with a 1M context window.

How can I reduce my API costs?

(1) Use batch pricing for non-urgent workloads — 50% discount. (2) Enable prompt caching — cached input tokens cost 50% less. (3) Route simple queries to cheaper models (GPT-5 Nano, GPT-4.1 Nano). (4) Limit output tokens with max_tokens. (5) Compress long prompts and prune conversation history.

What is the difference between batch and real-time pricing?

Batch API processes jobs asynchronously with a 24-hour turnaround window at 50% of real-time pricing. Ideal for evaluation, classification, embedding, and non-interactive workloads. Real-time API is for chat, copilots, and interactive apps where latency matters.

When should I use GPT-4.1 vs GPT-5?

GPT-4.1 family offers 1M-token context windows at competitive prices — ideal for long-document analysis, full-codebase reviews, and large knowledge-base RAG. GPT-5 family has stronger reasoning and instruction-following. For most cost-sensitive production, GPT-5 Mini hits the sweet spot; for maximum context at low cost, GPT-4.1 Nano is compelling.

What is the o-series and when should I use it?

The o-series (o3, o4 Mini) are reasoning models optimized for multi-step logic, math, coding, and scientific analysis. They "think" before responding, using more output tokens but producing higher-quality results for complex tasks. Use o3 for hard reasoning; o4 Mini for cost-effective reasoning workloads.

How does prompt caching work and how much can it save?

OpenAI automatically caches prompt prefixes longer than 1024 tokens. Cached input tokens cost 50% less. Structure your prompts with a long, static system message at the beginning, and vary only the user query at the end. At 50% cache hit rate, input costs drop by 25%. At 80% hit rate, input costs drop by 40%.

tokens ──────────────────────────────────────────── Input: 1,000 tokens × $5.00/1M → $0.0050/req Output: 500 tokens × $30.00/1M → $0.0150/req ──────────────────────────────────────────── Per request:
